Motion To Deem Matters Admitted And To Deem Documents Genuine Under C.C.P. 2033. 280(B) And For Monetary Sanctions Against Defendant Ricardo Dias Under C.C.P. 2023.030(A) And 2033.280(C)
Off Calendar Mar-22-2024 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances via Zoom Conference
Ricardo Diaz (sued as Ricardo Dias), 415-559-8668, Defendant in Pro Per
No other appearances.

The motion was not properly contested by Defendant Ricardo Diaz. With the assistance of Javier Bastidas, Esq., who appeared in an unrelated matter, the Court informs defendant that Plaintiff's motion is denied. No hearing/argument is held.

The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

PLAINTIFF DASF VAN NESS, LLC, A CALIFORNIA LIMITES LIABILITY Motion To Deem Matters Admitted And To Deem Documents Genuine Under C.C.P. 2033. 280(B) And For Monetary Sanctions Against Defendant Ricardo Dias Under C.C.P. 2023.030(A) And 2033.280(C)

Plaintiff claims ownership and management of a multi-unit residential building in San Francisco. The defendant, appearing in propria persona, occupies one of the units, No. 3. Plaintiff contends that any entitlement defendant had to continue his tenancy has expired, but he refuses to vacate the unit. The defendant, who does not speak English, contends that he enjoys a legitimate tenancy not dependent on any conditions or expired documents. He denies plaintiff's allegation he has not paid rent for his unit, at least through 2019.

From the record, it appears that plaintiff served a comprehensive group of written discovery items on defendant: interrogatories, a request for admissions and a request for production of documents. In the instant motion, plaintiff contends the request for admissions should be deemed admitted because defendant failed to respond. Plaintiff also contends a document should be deemed genuine, again because defendant failed to respond to the related discovery. Sanctions are also sought.

However, in response to the pending motion by plaintiff, defendant submitted copies of responses to all plaintiff's discovery items, including the request for admissions. He did so with the assistance of a tenant-advocacy group, serving the responses electronically a couple of days after plaintiff's motion to deem admissions was filed. It is of interest that plaintiff only addressed the request for admissions in its motion, apparently accepting the sufficiency of defendant's other discovery responses, timely or not.

Given the defendant's pro per status and the submission of competent and comprehensive responses to all of plaintiff's discovery items, the motion to deem the request for admissions admitted (several of which are simply negatively phrased legal conclusions) and the letter deemed genuine, and for related sanctions, is DENIED.

Judge Pro Tem Philip Ward is to prepare an order.

Judge Pro Tem: Philip Ward; Clerk: W. Trupek; Not Reported (302/JPT)
